name: Palantir
tagline: SAS wins on transparency, open standards, and self-service analytics for regulated industries

overview.whatTheyDo: Data integration and AI platform with Foundry (enterprise) and Gotham (government/defense). Known for ontology-based data modeling, operational workflows, and AI/ML deployment via AIP (Artificial Intelligence Platform).
overview.sweetSpot: Government defense and intelligence. Large enterprises needing complex data integration across siloed systems. Organizations wanting operational AI with human-in-the-loop workflows.
overview.typicalBuyer: CEO/COO (top-down), Head of Digital Transformation, Government CIO
overview.phPresence: No direct PH presence. Selling through US government partnerships and direct enterprise engagement. Limited ASEAN footprint outside Singapore defense contracts.
overview.recentMomentum: AIP (AI Platform) launch driving commercial growth. Stock surge on AI hype. Strong US government revenue. Expanding into healthcare and commercial sectors.
overview.pricingModel: Custom enterprise pricing (typically $1M+ annual contracts). Long implementation cycles with embedded consultants. High switching costs by design.

whereWeWin.headline: Transparency & Open Standards
whereWeWin.explanation: SAS uses open, documented algorithms with full explainability. Palantir's ontology and models operate as a black box — customers depend on Palantir consultants to understand their own data. For regulated industries, this opacity is a compliance risk.
whereWeWin.proofPoint: Philippine regulators (BSP, IC) require model explainability — Palantir cannot provide the audit trail format BSP examiners expect.

whereWeWin.headline: Self-Service for Business Users
whereWeWin.explanation: SAS enables business analysts to build and deploy analytics without coding. Palantir requires dedicated Palantir engineers (Forward Deployed Engineers) embedded at the customer. This creates dependency and inflates long-term costs.
whereWeWin.proofPoint: 60% of SAS Viya users are non-programmers. Palantir typically requires 5-10 embedded engineers per deployment.

whereWeWin.headline: Industry Solutions Out-of-the-Box
whereWeWin.explanation: SAS delivers pre-built fraud, AML, risk, and clinical solutions with Philippine regulatory compliance built in. Palantir is a horizontal platform — everything must be custom-built by their engineering team at significant cost.
whereWeWin.proofPoint: SAS Fraud Management deploys in weeks with pre-built detection models. Palantir fraud solutions require 6-12 months of custom development.

whereTheyWin.whyItMatters: Palantir's ontology approach excels at connecting highly disparate data sources into a unified operational model. Strong for organizations with 50+ siloed systems.
whereTheyWin.howToNeutralize: SAS Data Management handles complex integration. For most PH enterprises, the data landscape is simpler than Palantir's sweet spot. Ask: 'How many data sources are we talking about?' If <20, SAS handles it without Palantir complexity.

whereTheyWin.whyItMatters: Palantir has strong credentials with US and allied defense/intelligence agencies. This can influence government deals through top-down relationships.
whereTheyWin.howToNeutralize: SAS has decades of civilian government analytics experience (tax, fraud, healthcare). In PH, the need is civilian analytics — not defense intelligence. SAS's regulatory and public sector track record is more relevant.

whereTheyWin.whyItMatters: AIP enables non-technical users to interact with AI through natural language and operational workflows. Strong UX for decision-makers.
whereTheyWin.howToNeutralize: SAS Intelligent Decisioning provides similar operational AI with the added benefit of governance, audit trails, and regulatory compliance. AIP is new and unproven in regulated environments.

features.notes: Palantir lacks formal model governance for regulated industries
Palantir requires embedded engineers for most operations
Palantir's ontology is uniquely strong for complex environments
SAS sub-10ms scoring vs. Palantir's workflow-based approach
Palantir's visual analytics for graph/network data is strong
AIP's natural language interaction is ahead of SAS current offerings
Both support on-prem for data sovereignty
Palantir pricing is opaque and typically $1M+ annual minimum
SAS AutoML is mature; Palantir's is emerging
SAS has 50 years of validated statistical procedures

landmines.question: Can your team operate the platform independently — without vendor engineers on-site?
landmines.expectedAnswer: Palantir deployments typically require Forward Deployed Engineers
landmines.whatToSayNext: SAS enables your team to be self-sufficient after training. No ongoing dependency on embedded vendor consultants.

landmines.question: How would you demonstrate model decisions to BSP during an examination?
landmines.expectedAnswer: Palantir can't produce BSP-format model documentation
landmines.whatToSayNext: SAS Model Manager auto-generates regulatory documentation in the format BSP examiners expect.

landmines.question: What's the total cost including implementation consultants for the first 3 years?
landmines.expectedAnswer: Palantir TCO is typically 3-5x the license cost due to embedded engineers
landmines.whatToSayNext: SAS total cost is predictable — license + standard implementation. No ongoing consultant dependency.

objections.objection: Palantir's AI Platform (AIP) is more advanced
objections.whyTheySayIt: AIP demo is impressive. Natural language interface is compelling to executives.
objections.response: AIP is impressive for ad-hoc queries and operational workflows. But for production ML in regulated industries — where you need governance, explainability, audit trails, and regulatory compliance — SAS provides what AIP cannot. Ask: 'Will BSP accept an AIP-generated decision as auditable?'
proofPoints: Palantir has zero Philippine banking references for regulated analytics
BSP requires documented model lifecycle that AIP doesn't provide
objections.whatNotToSay: Don't dismiss AIP as 'just a chatbot' — it's genuinely innovative. Acknowledge it and pivot to governance.

objections.objection: Palantir is used by the biggest organizations in the world
objections.whyTheySayIt: Brand prestige and perceived safety. US government adoption impresses executives.
objections.response: Palantir excels in defense and intelligence — where budgets are unlimited and data is classified. In civilian regulated industries — banking, insurance, healthcare — SAS has 50 years of proven deployments. The question is: which platform was built for YOUR industry's specific requirements?
proofPoints: SAS serves 9 of top 10 global banks
Palantir's commercial revenue is growing but concentrated in US
objections.whatNotToSay: Don't attack their government credentials — respect them and redirect to commercial/regulated context.

phContext.localPresence: No Philippines office or team. Nearest presence is Singapore. Would require direct engagement from US/Singapore team or through a systems integrator.
knownCustomers: Some government interest through defense channels
phContext.localPricing: Enterprise-only pricing starting at $1M+ annually. Not accessible for mid-market PH organizations. No marketplace or flexible consumption model.
regulatoryGaps: No pre-built fraud or AML solutions for PH market
Model explainability doesn't meet PH regulatory format requirements
Forward Deployed Engineer dependency creates vendor lock-in risk

partnerWhen: Customer has complex data integration needs across 50+ systems (government scale)
Defense or intelligence use case where Palantir has clearance and SAS doesn't
Palantir handles data layer, SAS handles analytics and compliance layer
competeWhen: Customer needs self-service analytics (not embedded engineers)
Budget-conscious organization ($1M+ Palantir minimum is too high)
Philippine regulatory compliance is a primary requirement

proofPoints.title: Different market positioning
proofPoints.detail: Gartner positions Palantir in Decision Intelligence, while SAS leads in Advanced Analytics and AI — different categories serving different needs.

proofPoints.title: SAS self-sufficiency vs. Palantir dependency
proofPoints.detail: Organizations report 12-18 month timelines to reduce Palantir consultant dependency. SAS customers are typically self-sufficient within 3-6 months.

proofPoints.title: TCO comparison at 3-year mark
proofPoints.detail: Palantir TCO (license + embedded engineers + custom development) typically 2-3x SAS TCO for equivalent regulated analytics use cases.
